item.title: 【Hadoop on AmpereOne Reference Architecture】AmpereOne 参考架构上的 Hadoop
item.description:
【

Ampere processors with Arm architecture deliver superior power efficiency and cost advantages compared to traditional x86 architecture. Hadoop, with its core components and broader ecosystem, is fully compatible with Arm-based platforms. 

Ampere Computing has previously published a comprehensive reference architecture demonstrating Hadoop deployments on Ampere® Altra® M processors. This paper builds on that foundation and extends the analysis by highlighting Hadoop performance on the next generation of AmpereOne® M processor.

】

与传统 x86 架构相比，采用 Arm 架构的 Ampere 处理器具有卓越的功效和成本优势。 Hadoop 凭借其核心组件和更广泛的生态系统，与基于 Arm 的平台完全兼容。 

Ampere Computer 之前发布了一个全面的参考架构，展示了 Ampere® Altra® M 处理器上的 Hadoop 部署。本文以此为基础，并通过重点介绍下一代 AmpereOne® M 处理器上的 Hadoop 性能来扩展分析。

item.title: 【5 Proven Strategies for Modernizing Legacy .NET Applications】遗留 .NET 应用程序现代化的 5 个行之有效的策略
item.description:
【

As a developer, it is almost inevitable that you’ll eventually work on maintaining or modernizing an existing codebase. Rarely is this straightforward; the challenge can feel overwhelming, especially if the core logic is a tangled mess and a classic ‘big ball of mud’. This lack of clarity decreases your confidence and increases the risk of the system breaking in unexpected places whenever you make changes.

Defining Legacy .NET Code

A common misconception is that legacy code must be old code. While age can be a factor, a system might use modern frameworks but still be poorly organized and patched together to meet a deadline. While many factors define legacy code, we’ll focus on two of the biggest ones today.

】

作为一名开发人员，您最终几乎不可避免地要致力于维护或现代化现有代码库。这很少是直截了当的。挑战可能会让人感到难以承受，尤其是当核心逻辑是一团混乱和一个经典的“大泥球”时。这种缺乏清晰度会降低您的信心，并增加您进行更改时系统在意外位置崩溃的风险。

定义旧版 .NET 代码

一个常见的误解是遗留代码必须是旧代码。虽然年龄可能是一个因素，但系统可能使用现代框架，但仍然组织不善，无法在截止日期前拼凑在一起。虽然定义遗留代码的因素有很多，但今天我们将重点关注其中两个最重要的因素。

item.title: 【Chat with Your Oracle Database: SQLcl MCP + GitHub Copilot】与您的 Oracle 数据库聊天：SQLcl MCP + GitHub Copilot
item.description:
【

Ask questions in plain English inside VS Code. Get SQL results back instantly — no copy-pasting, no switching tools.

The Problem: Too Many Switches

If you work with Oracle databases, you know the drill: write SQL in a text editor, copy it to SQL Developer or SQLcl, run it, then copy results back. Add an AI assistant into the mix and you get another window — one that can write SQL but has no way to actually run it against your database.

】

在 VS Code 中用简单的英语提问。立即获取 SQL 结果 - 无需复制粘贴，无需切换工具。

问题：开关太多

如果您使用 Oracle 数据库，您就会知道具体操作：在文本编辑器中编写 SQL，将其复制到 SQL Developer 或 SQLcl，运行它，然后将结果复制回来。添加一个人工智能助手，你会得到另一个窗口 - 一个可以编写 SQL 但无法针对数据库实际运行它的窗口。

item.title: 【Not AI-First — Work-First!】不是人工智能优先——工作优先！
item.description:
【

As the AI marketing machine accelerates, a new vocabulary has emerged alongside it. One of the most popular current phrases is “AI-First.” Organizations aspire to become AI-First enterprises.

Software teams aim for AI-First development. Strategies are increasingly framed through an AI-First lens.

】

随着人工智能营销机器的加速发展，新的词汇也随之出现。当前最流行的短语之一是“AI-First”。组织渴望成为人工智能优先的企业。

软件团队的目标是人工智能优先的开发。战略越来越多地通过人工智能优先的视角来制定。

item.title: 【Retiring a Tier-0 Legacy Database Without Breaking the Business】在不影响业务的情况下淘汰 Tier-0 旧数据库
item.description:
【

The most dangerous lie in tier-0 migrations is not “this will be quick.” It is “we can always roll back.”

That sentence sounds reasonable when you are migrating a stateless service or swapping a cache. It becomes fiction when the system you are retiring holds years of operational history, audit-critical records, dispute workflows, and the kind of long-tail queries that only show up when something goes wrong at the worst possible time. In 2026, the pressure is higher because data retention horizons keep stretching while reliability expectations keep tightening. You are not migrating a database. You are migrating accountability.

】

第 0 层迁移中最危险的谎言不是“这会很快”。这是“我们总是可以回滚。”

当您迁移无状态服务或交换缓存时，这句话听起来很合理。当您即将退役的系统拥有多年的运行历史、审计关键记录、争议工作流程以及仅在最糟糕的时间出现问题时才会出现的长尾查询时，这一切就变得虚构了。到 2026 年，压力会更大，因为数据保留期限不断延长，而可靠性预期不断收紧。您不是在迁移数据库。您正在迁移责任。

item.title: 【From Concept to Production: A Strategic Framework for AI/ML Project Success】从概念到生产：AI/ML 项目成功的战略框架
item.description:
【

The AI revolution has promised to transform business operations by automating manual work intelligently; however, it has fallen short of expectations.

MIT recently published a report claiming 95% of generative AI projects fail to deliver measurable ROI. This highlights a massive gap between AI hype and real-world success. When you compare this with the Project Management Institute (PMI) findings that 73.4% of traditional projects succeed, the difference is hard to ignore. This is not just a simple efficiency issue. This is a fundamental crisis in how we build AI systems. 

】

人工智能革命有望通过智能自动化手动工作来改变业务运营；然而，它没有达到预期。

麻省理工学院最近发布了一份报告，声称 95% 的生成式 AI 项目未能提供可衡量的投资回报率。这突显了人工智能炒作与现实世界的成功之间的巨大差距。当将此与项目管理协会 (PMI) 的调查结果（73.4% 的传统项目成功）进行比较时，差异不容忽视。这不仅仅是一个简单的效率问题。这是我们构建人工智能系统的根本危机。

item.title: 【Reducing Deployment Time by 60% on GCP: A CI/CD Pipeline Redesign Case Study】将 GCP 上的部署时间缩短 60%：CI/CD 管道重新设计案例研究
item.description:
【

The Problem: Deployments Were Slowing Down Engineering

Our deployment cycle had quietly become a bottleneck.

Every production release took 45–60 minutes, even for small changes. That delay created hesitation around shipping frequently. Engineers batched features instead of releasing incrementally. Rollbacks were painful. Incident response was slower than it should have been.

】

问题：部署减慢了工程进度

我们的部署周期已悄然成为瓶颈。

每个生产版本都需要 45–60 分钟，即使是很小的更改。这种延误经常导致人们对运输犹豫不决。工程师批量发布功能而不是增量发布。回滚是痛苦的。事件响应速度比应有的速度要慢。

item.title: 【How Our gRPC Services Collapsed During Traffic Bursts — and What Finally Stopped It】我们的 gRPC 服务如何在流量爆发期间崩溃 - 以及最终阻止它的原因
item.description:
【

Traffic bursts don’t look dangerous at first. Most of the time, dashboards are still mostly green while the system is already drifting toward failure. That’s exactly what kept happening to us.

For a long time, our services behaved predictably. Traffic grew gradually. Autoscaling worked. Latency stayed reasonable. Things felt stable. Then we started seeing short, sharp traffic bursts — often triggered by promotions or external events. Request rates jumped within seconds. Autoscaling never had time to react. What followed became uncomfortably familiar.

】

流量激增乍一看并不危险。大多数时候，仪表板仍然大部分是绿色的，而系统已经逐渐走向失败。这正是我们不断发生的事情。

长期以来，我们的服务表现都符合预期。客流量逐渐增长。自动缩放有效。延迟保持合理。事情感觉很稳定。然后我们开始看到短暂而急剧的流量爆发——通常是由促销或外部事件引发的。请求率在几秒钟内跃升。自动缩放从来没有时间做出反应。接下来发生的事情变得令人不安的熟悉。

item.title: 【How Spec-Driven Development Brings Structure to AI-Assisted Engineering and How We Put It to the Test】规范驱动开发如何为人工智能辅助工程带来结构以及我们如何对其进行测试
item.description:
【

AI coding assistants have made developers incredibly fast since the start of the AI boom, but this new speed often comes at a hidden cost. The IT industry is realizing that generating code is the easy part. The real challenge is building systems that are coherent, maintainable, and actually do what they were supposed to do.

This is where Spec-Driven Development (SDD) comes in. This methodology shifts the focus from vibe coding to following the general intent, using specifications as the new source of truth for AI-assisted engineering.

】自人工智能热潮开始以来，人工智能编码助手使开发人员的速度变得令人难以置信，但这种新的速度往往会带来隐性成本。 IT 行业正在意识到生成代码是最容易的部分。真正的挑战是构建连贯的、可维护的、并且真正做他们应该做的事情的系统。

这就是规范驱动开发 (SDD) 的用武之地。这种方法将重点从振动编码转移到遵循一般意图，使用规范作为人工智能辅助工程的新事实来源。

item.title: 【AI-Generated DataWeave in MuleSoft: Production Failure Modes and How to Make It Safe】MuleSoft 中人工智能生成的 DataWeave：生产故障模式以及如何确保其安全
item.description:
【

Generative AI can produce DataWeave transformations in seconds. For teams under delivery pressure, that looks like a productivity multiplier: paste a payload, describe the mapping, and a seemingly valid script appears.

I’ve personally reviewed multiple integration flows where the generated mapping looked perfectly reasonable in isolation, but failed within hours of deployment once retries and partial payloads entered the picture. These failures typically surface only under load, partial payloads, or replay scenarios — and they have real operational and business consequences: higher runtime cost, increased incident toil, and data integrity problems. This article documents the failure patterns we repeatedly see in real systems and provides pragmatic, code-level guardrails to make AI-assisted DataWeave safe for production.

】

生成式 AI 可以在几秒钟内生成 DataWeave 转换。对于面临交付压力的团队来说，这看起来像是生产力倍增器：粘贴有效负载，描述映射，然后就会出现看似有效的脚本。

我亲自审查了多个集成流程，其中生成的映射单独看起来完全合理，但一旦重试和部分有效负载进入图片，部署后数小时内就会失败。这些故障通常仅在负载、部分有效负载或重放场景下才会出现，并且它们会产生实际的操作和业务后果：更高的运行时成本、增加的事故负担和数据完整性问题。本文记录了我们在实际系统中反复看到的故障模式，并提供了务实的代码级护栏，使 AI 辅助的 DataWeave 能够安全生产。
